reasoning: The 360-day average of fatalities leading up to March 2026 is 14.25, meaning to exceed it, at least 15 fatalities are required in the 30 days prior to March 22, 2026. This threshold was heavily inflated by a single large event in the past 360 days: mass protests against fuel price hikes in July 2025, which claimed 22 to 30 lives. Conflict fatality distributions are notoriously right-skewed, meaning the median baseline month has fewer fatalities than the mean month, especially when a massive rare event sits in the average. With 23 out of the 30 days in the target window already passed (Feb 20 – Mar 15) and exhaustive English and Portuguese searches turning up no reports of significant single or multi-fatality events in Angola (from clashes, riots, or FLEC separatist violence in Cabinda), it is quite unlikely that the baseline scattered violence will sum up to 15 or more fatalities. Therefore, the chance of the final 30 days exceeding the 360-day average is low.
